response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with broad polyglot experience, but the resume shows minimal recent work in the JVM ecosystem. Java appears only briefly (NI LabWindows/CVI in C/C++, and some robotic arm work years ago), with no demonstrated Kotlin, Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or IDE-internals experience. The closest adjacent experience is C/C++ work on NI LabWindows/CVI (a C IDE), but that predates a decade of mostly Node.js/TypeScript/DLang work. For a role requiring deep JVM and Spring expertise plus genuine interest in IDE/language tooling, the fit is weak.
strengths: Long track record across web, mobile, desktop and embedded systems with strong software-craftsmanship habits (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
Prior IDE-adjacent experience writing C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI
Demonstrated ability to design and ship complex, large-scale systems (high-throughput services at Sociomantic, GIS platform, pricing systems)
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin experience and only peripheral, dated Java exposure
No evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or other enterprise JVM framework work
No clear signal of interest or experience in IDE internals or JVM language tooling

key_factors.factor: Lack of Spring/JVM enterprise framework experience
Prior C/C++ work on an IDE product (LabWindows/CVI)
